notes: ML classifier (ONNX) trained on Python MediaPipe features, tested in browser via ONNX Runtime Web. ML Only mode replaces categorical scoring entirely. Model: 343KB ONNX, 142-dim statistical features, MLP 256→128→64, trained with 100 augmentations per sample. CV accuracy was 99.2% but LOO was 0% (1 sample per class). Browser test gets 11.8% — likely limited by JS/Python MediaPipe feature mismatch.
changes: ONNX model trained from Python MediaPipe landmarks (extract_and_train.py)
142-dim statistical feature vector: per-dim stats (7), trajectory features (10), non-dom hand (3), body-relative distances (9), metadata (1)
MLP architecture: 256→128→64 with BatchNorm, ReLU, Dropout(0.3)
100 augmentations per sample (noise, scale, translation, speed warp, finger flip, frame dropout)
ONNX Runtime Web for browser inference
ML Only mode: replaces categorical scoring with ML prediction from last segment

comparedToV13.note: Completely different pass sets — ML discriminates on different features than categorical scoring
